location: Received from Finger Reader Enia at Roundtable Hold by trading the Elden Remembrance earned from defeating Radagon of the Golden Order and the Elden Beast — the two-phase final boss atop the Erdtree in Leyndell, Ashen Capital.

source.name: Radagon of the Golden Order / Elden Beast (Elden Remembrance)

tactics: This is the game's final boss — a genuine two-phase fight (Radagon, then immediately the Elden Beast) with no grace to rest at in between.

At Roundtable Hold, trading the Elden Remembrance forces a choice between Marika's Hammer and the Sacred Relic Sword — you can only pick one per Remembrance unless you duplicate it with a Walking Mausoleum's Stonesword Key mechanic.
Radagon has fast, wide-tracking melee combos; the Elden Beast that follows has huge sweeping AoE attacks — use the arena's open space to create dodge room.
lore: The stone hammer forged in the distant land of the Numen, the very tool Queen Marika used to shatter the Elden Ring — and that Radagon later wielded trying to mend it, leaving it fused with fragments of rune.

builds.name: Endgame Faith/Quality Hybrid
builds.why: D/D/D scaling across Strength, Dexterity and Faith plus the high-damage Gold Breaker leap attack suit a late-game Faith-quality build looking for one big finishing hit.
